  • 2-2) Valid Palindrome (LC 125) — Two Pointers
  • 2-3) Palindromic Substrings (LC 647) — Count All Palindromes
  • 2-4) Palindrome Number (LC 9) — Math Reversal
  • 2-5) Valid Palindrome II (LC 680) — Two Pointers with One Skip
  • 2-6) Palindrome Linked List (LC 234) — Fast/Slow Pointer + Reverse
  • 2-7) Longest Palindromic Subsequence (LC 516) — Interval DP
  • 2-8) Minimum Insertion Steps to Make a String Palindrome (LC 1312) — Interval DP
  • 2-9) Palindrome Partitioning (LC 131) — Backtracking
  • 2-10) Palindrome Partitioning II (LC 132) — DP
  • 2-11) Find Longest Awesome Substring (LC 1542) — Bitmask + Prefix XOR

Palindrome (回文)

Overview

Palindrome is a sequence that reads the same forward and backward. It’s a fundamental concept in string manipulation and array processing, commonly appearing in coding interviews.

Key Properties

  • Time Complexity: O(n) for basic operations, O(n²) for substring problems
  • Space Complexity: O(1) for two-pointer approach, O(n) for recursive/DP solutions
  • Core Idea: Compare characters/elements from both ends moving toward the center
  • When to Use: String validation, substring problems, number manipulation, partitioning

Problem Categories

Pattern 1: Basic Palindrome Validation

  • Description: Check if a given string/array/number is a palindrome
  • Examples: LC 9, 125, 234 - Valid Palindrome, Palindrome Number, Palindrome Linked List
  • Pattern: Two pointers from ends, or reverse and compare

Pattern 2: Palindrome Substring Problems

  • Description: Find longest palindromic substring or count palindromic substrings
  • Examples: LC 5, 647, 214 - Longest Palindromic Substring, Palindromic Substrings
  • Pattern: Center expansion or dynamic programming

Pattern 3: Palindrome Construction & Partitioning

  • Description: Break string into palindromic parts or construct palindromes
  • Examples: LC 131, 132, 336 - Palindrome Partitioning I/II, Palindrome Pairs
  • Pattern: Backtracking with palindrome validation, dynamic programming

Pattern 4: Palindrome Manipulation

  • Description: Transform strings to create palindromes (insertions, deletions)
  • Examples: LC 516, 1312, 1332 - Longest Palindromic Subsequence, Minimum Insertions
  • Pattern: Dynamic programming with edit distance concepts

Pattern 5: Advanced Palindrome Problems

  • Description: Complex palindrome problems with additional constraints
  • Examples: LC 1147, 1177, 1930 - Longest Chunked Palindrome, Can Make Palindrome
  • Pattern: Sliding window, hash tables, bit manipulation

Pattern 6: Number Palindromes

  • Description: Palindrome problems involving integers and mathematical operations
  • Examples: LC 9, 479, 564 - Palindrome Number, Largest Palindrome Product
  • Pattern: Mathematical manipulation, string conversion

References

  • LeetCode Palindrome Problems
  • Algorithm Design Manual - String Algorithms

Templates & Algorithms

Template Comparison Table

Template Type Use Case Time Complexity When to Use
Two Pointers Basic validation O(n) Simple palindrome check
Center Expansion Find longest substring O(n²) Substring problems
Reverse & Compare Simple check O(n) When space is not a constraint
DP (2D) Complex substring counting O(n²) Multiple subproblems
Recursive + Memo Subsequence problems O(n²) Overlapping subproblems
Backtracking Partitioning O(2^n) Generate all partitions

Template 1: Two Pointers (Most Common)

1def is_palindrome_two_pointers(s):
2    """
3    Basic palindrome check using two pointers
4    Time: O(n), Space: O(1)
5    """
6    left, right = 0, len(s) - 1
7    
8    while left < right:
9        # Skip non-alphanumeric characters (for valid palindrome problems)
10        while left < right and not s[left].isalnum():
11            left += 1
12        while left < right and not s[right].isalnum():
13            right -= 1
14            
15        # Compare characters (case-insensitive)
16        if s[left].lower() != s[right].lower():
17            return False
18            
19        left += 1
20        right -= 1
21    
22    return True
23
24# Java version
25class Solution {
26    public boolean isPalindrome(String s) {
27        int left = 0, right = s.length() - 1;
28        
29        while (left < right) {
30            while (left < right && !Character.isLetterOrDigit(s.charAt(left))) {
31                left++;
32            }
33            while (left < right && !Character.isLetterOrDigit(s.charAt(right))) {
34                right--;
35            }
36            
37            if (Character.toLowerCase(s.charAt(left)) != 
38                Character.toLowerCase(s.charAt(right))) {
39                return false;
40            }
41            
42            left++;
43            right--;
44        }
45        
46        return true;
47    }
48}

Template 2: Center Expansion (Substring Problems)

1def longest_palindromic_substring(s):
2    """
3    Find longest palindromic substring using center expansion
4    Time: O(n²), Space: O(1)
5    """
6    if not s:
7        return ""
8    
9    start, max_len = 0, 1
10    
11    def expand_around_center(left, right):
12        # Expand while characters match
13        while left >= 0 and right < len(s) and s[left] == s[right]:
14            left -= 1
15            right += 1
16        return right - left - 1  # Length of palindrome
17    
18    for i in range(len(s)):
19        # Check for odd-length palindromes (center at i)
20        len1 = expand_around_center(i, i)
21        # Check for even-length palindromes (center between i and i+1)
22        len2 = expand_around_center(i, i + 1)
23        
24        current_max = max(len1, len2)
25        if current_max > max_len:
26            max_len = current_max
27            start = i - (current_max - 1) // 2
28    
29    return s[start:start + max_len]
30
31# Java version
32class Solution {
33    public String longestPalindrome(String s) {
34        if (s == null || s.length() < 1) return "";
35        
36        int start = 0, end = 0;
37        
38        for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
39            int len1 = expandAroundCenter(s, i, i);     // odd length
40            int len2 = expandAroundCenter(s, i, i + 1); // even length
41            int len = Math.max(len1, len2);
42            
43            if (len > end - start) {
44                start = i - (len - 1) / 2;
45                end = i + len / 2;
46            }
47        }
48        
49        return s.substring(start, end + 1);
50    }
51    
52    private int expandAroundCenter(String s, int left, int right) {
53        while (left >= 0 && right < s.length() && s.charAt(left) == s.charAt(right)) {
54            left--;
55            right++;
56        }
57        return right - left - 1;
58    }
59}

Template 3: Dynamic Programming (Complex Counting)

1def count_palindromic_substrings(s):
2    """
3    Count all palindromic substrings using DP
4    Time: O(n²), Space: O(n²)
5    """
6    n = len(s)
7    # dp[i][j] = True if s[i:j+1] is palindrome
8    dp = [[False] * n for _ in range(n)]
9    count = 0
10    
11    # Single characters are palindromes
12    for i in range(n):
13        dp[i][i] = True
14        count += 1
15    
16    # Check for palindromes of length 2
17    for i in range(n - 1):
18        if s[i] == s[i + 1]:
19            dp[i][i + 1] = True
20            count += 1
21    
22    # Check for palindromes of length 3+
23    for length in range(3, n + 1):
24        for i in range(n - length + 1):
25            j = i + length - 1
26            
27            # Check if s[i:j+1] is palindrome
28            if s[i] == s[j] and dp[i + 1][j - 1]:
29                dp[i][j] = True
30                count += 1
31    
32    return count
33
34# Space-optimized version using center expansion
35def count_palindromic_substrings_optimized(s):
36    """
37    Time: O(n²), Space: O(1)
38    """
39    count = 0
40    
41    def expand_and_count(left, right):
42        nonlocal count
43        while left >= 0 and right < len(s) and s[left] == s[right]:
44            count += 1
45            left -= 1
46            right += 1
47    
48    for i in range(len(s)):
49        # Odd length palindromes
50        expand_and_count(i, i)
51        # Even length palindromes
52        expand_and_count(i, i + 1)
53    
54    return count

Template 4: Backtracking (Partitioning)

1def palindrome_partitioning(s):
2    """
3    Find all palindrome partitions using backtracking
4    Time: O(n * 2^n), Space: O(n)
5    """
6    result = []
7    current_partition = []
8    
9    def is_palindrome(start, end):
10        while start < end:
11            if s[start] != s[end]:
12                return False
13            start += 1
14            end -= 1
15        return True
16    
17    def backtrack(start):
18        # Base case: reached end of string
19        if start >= len(s):
20            result.append(current_partition[:])
21            return
22        
23        # Try all possible endings for current substring
24        for end in range(start, len(s)):
25            # If current substring is palindrome
26            if is_palindrome(start, end):
27                current_partition.append(s[start:end + 1])
28                backtrack(end + 1)
29                current_partition.pop()
30    
31    backtrack(0)
32    return result
33
34# Optimized with memoization
35def palindrome_partitioning_memo(s):
36    """
37    With palindrome check memoization
38    """
39    n = len(s)
40    # Precompute palindrome checks
41    is_palin = [[False] * n for _ in range(n)]
42    
43    # Fill palindrome table
44    for i in range(n):
45        is_palin[i][i] = True
46    
47    for i in range(n - 1):
48        is_palin[i][i + 1] = (s[i] == s[i + 1])
49    
50    for length in range(3, n + 1):
51        for i in range(n - length + 1):
52            j = i + length - 1
53            is_palin[i][j] = (s[i] == s[j] and is_palin[i + 1][j - 1])
54    
55    result = []
56    current_partition = []
57    
58    def backtrack(start):
59        if start >= n:
60            result.append(current_partition[:])
61            return
62        
63        for end in range(start, n):
64            if is_palin[start][end]:
65                current_partition.append(s[start:end + 1])
66                backtrack(end + 1)
67                current_partition.pop()
68    
69    backtrack(0)
70    return result

Template 5: Longest Palindromic Subsequence (DP)

1def longest_palindromic_subsequence(s):
2    """
3    Find length of longest palindromic subsequence using DP
4    Time: O(n²), Space: O(n²)
5    """
6    n = len(s)
7    # dp[i][j] = length of LPS in s[i:j+1]
8    dp = [[0] * n for _ in range(n)]
9    
10    # Single characters have LPS length 1
11    for i in range(n):
12        dp[i][i] = 1
13    
14    # Fill for substrings of length 2+
15    for length in range(2, n + 1):
16        for i in range(n - length + 1):
17            j = i + length - 1
18            
19            if s[i] == s[j]:
20                dp[i][j] = dp[i + 1][j - 1] + 2
21            else:
22                dp[i][j] = max(dp[i + 1][j], dp[i][j - 1])
23    
24    return dp[0][n - 1]
25
26# Space-optimized version
27def longest_palindromic_subsequence_optimized(s):
28    """
29    Time: O(n²), Space: O(n)
30    """
31    n = len(s)
32    dp = [1] * n
33    
34    for i in range(n - 2, -1, -1):
35        prev = 0
36        for j in range(i + 1, n):
37            temp = dp[j]
38            if s[i] == s[j]:
39                dp[j] = prev + 2
40            else:
41                dp[j] = max(dp[j], dp[j - 1])
42            prev = temp
43    
44    return dp[n - 1]

Template 6: Number Palindrome

1def is_palindrome_number(x):
2    """
3    Check if integer is palindrome without converting to string
4    Time: O(log x), Space: O(1)
5    """
6    # Negative numbers are not palindromes
7    if x < 0:
8        return False
9    
10    # Single digit numbers are palindromes
11    if x < 10:
12        return True
13    
14    # Numbers ending in 0 (except 0) are not palindromes
15    if x % 10 == 0:
16        return False
17    
18    reversed_half = 0
19    
20    # Reverse only half of the number
21    while x > reversed_half:
22        reversed_half = reversed_half * 10 + x % 10
23        x //= 10
24    
25    # For even length: x == reversed_half
26    # For odd length: x == reversed_half // 10
27    return x == reversed_half or x == reversed_half // 10
28
29# Java version
30class Solution {
31    public boolean isPalindrome(int x) {
32        if (x < 0 || (x % 10 == 0 && x != 0)) {
33            return false;
34        }
35        
36        int reversedHalf = 0;
37        while (x > reversedHalf) {
38            reversedHalf = reversedHalf * 10 + x % 10;
39            x /= 10;
40        }
41        
42        return x == reversedHalf || x == reversedHalf / 10;
43    }
44}

Problems by Pattern

Pattern 1: Basic Palindrome Validation

Problem LC # Key Technique Difficulty Template
Valid Palindrome 125 Two pointers, character filtering Easy Two Pointers
Palindrome Number 9 Mathematical reversal Easy Number Palindrome
Palindrome Linked List 234 Two pointers, reverse half Easy Two Pointers
Valid Palindrome II 680 Two pointers, one deletion Easy Two Pointers

Pattern 2: Palindrome Substring Problems

Problem LC # Key Technique Difficulty Template
Longest Palindromic Substring 5 Center expansion Medium Center Expansion
Palindromic Substrings 647 Center expansion/DP Medium Center Expansion
Shortest Palindrome 214 KMP/Center expansion Hard Center Expansion
Longest Palindromic Subsequence 516 DP (2D) Medium DP Subsequence
Palindromic Substring Queries 1177 Bit manipulation Medium Advanced
Minimum Insertion Steps to Make Palindrome 1312 DP (edit distance) Hard DP Subsequence

Pattern 3: Palindrome Construction & Partitioning

Problem LC # Key Technique Difficulty Template
Palindrome Partitioning 131 Backtracking + palindrome check Medium Backtracking
Palindrome Partitioning II 132 DP + palindrome preprocessing Hard DP + Backtracking
Palindrome Pairs 336 HashMap + string manipulation Hard Advanced
Valid Palindrome III 1216 DP (k deletions allowed) Hard DP Manipulation
Break a Palindrome 1328 Greedy + string manipulation Medium Greedy

Pattern 4: Palindrome Manipulation (Insertions/Deletions)

Problem LC # Key Technique Difficulty Template
Minimum Deletions to Make Palindrome 516* DP (LPS difference) Medium DP Subsequence
Minimum Insertions for Palindrome 1312 DP (edit distance) Hard DP Subsequence
Delete Operation for Two Strings 583* DP (LCS concept) Medium DP Subsequence
Longest Palindromic Subsequence II 1682 DP with constraints Medium DP Subsequence
Count Different Palindromic Subsequences 730 DP with character counting Hard DP Advanced

Pattern 5: Advanced Palindrome Problems

Problem LC # Key Technique Difficulty Template
Longest Chunked Palindrome Decomposition 1147 Greedy + two pointers Hard Advanced
Check If Word Is Valid After Substitutions 1003* Stack (palindrome-like pattern) Medium Advanced
Unique Length-3 Palindromic Subsequences 1930 Set + character analysis Medium Advanced
Maximum Product of Length of Palindromic Subsequences 1930* Bitmask + DP Hard Advanced
Find Palindrome With Fixed Length 2217 Mathematical construction Medium Number Palindrome

Pattern 6: Number Palindromes

Problem LC # Key Technique Difficulty Template
Palindrome Number 9 Mathematical reversal Easy Number Palindrome
Largest Palindrome Product 479 Mathematical construction Hard Number Palindrome
Super Palindromes 906 Mathematical enumeration Hard Number Palindrome
Closest Palindrome 564 Mathematical analysis Hard Number Palindrome
Palindromic Prime Numbers Custom Number theory Medium Number Palindrome

Additional Practice Problems

Problem LC # Key Technique Difficulty Template
Reverse String 344 Two pointers basics Easy Two Pointers
Reverse Words in a String III 557 Two pointers per word Easy Two Pointers
Find the Difference 389 Character counting Easy Advanced
Reverse Only Letters 917 Two pointers with filtering Easy Two Pointers
Valid Parentheses 20 Stack (symmetry concept) Easy Advanced
Longest Palindromic Path in Tree Custom Tree DP Hard Advanced
Palindrome Removal Custom Interval DP Hard DP Advanced
Count Palindromic Paths Custom Tree traversal Medium Advanced

Problem Categories Summary

  • Total Problems: 40+ classified problems
  • Easy: 8 problems (20%)
  • Medium: 20 problems (50%)
  • Hard: 12+ problems (30%)
  • Most Common Patterns: Center Expansion, Two Pointers, Dynamic Programming
  • Advanced Techniques: Backtracking, Bit manipulation, Mathematical construction

Template Usage Statistics

  • Two Pointers: 25% of problems
  • Center Expansion: 20% of problems
  • Dynamic Programming: 30% of problems
  • Backtracking: 10% of problems
  • Advanced/Hybrid: 15% of problems

Pattern Selection Framework

Decision Flowchart for Palindrome Problems

1Problem Analysis Flowchart:
2
31. Is this a simple validation problem (check if input is palindrome)?
4   ├── YES → Use Template 1: Two Pointers
5   │   ├── String/Array input → Two pointers from ends
6   │   ├── Linked List input → Two pointers + reverse half
7   │   └── Integer input → Template 6: Number Palindrome
8   └── NO → Continue to 2
9
102. Do you need to find/count palindromic substrings?
11   ├── YES → 
12   │   ├── Find longest substring → Template 2: Center Expansion
13   │   ├── Count all substrings → Template 2: Center Expansion (optimized)
14   │   └── Complex substring queries → Template 3: Dynamic Programming
15   └── NO → Continue to 3
16
173. Do you need to partition/construct palindromes?
18   ├── YES →
19   │   ├── Find all partitions → Template 4: Backtracking
20   │   ├── Minimum cuts needed → Template 3: DP + preprocessing
21   │   └── Construct specific palindrome → Advanced techniques
22   └── NO → Continue to 4
23
244. Do you need to modify string to make it palindrome?
25   ├── YES →
26   │   ├── Minimum insertions/deletions → Template 5: DP Subsequence
27   │   ├── With k operations allowed → Template 3: DP with constraints
28   │   └── Optimal transformation → Advanced DP techniques
29   └── NO → Continue to 5
30
315. Is this a number-based palindrome problem?
32   ├── YES → Template 6: Number Palindrome
33   │   ├── Check palindrome number → Mathematical reversal
34   │   ├── Generate palindromes → Mathematical construction
35   │   └── Complex number constraints → Advanced math
36   └── NO → Continue to 6
37
386. Advanced/Complex palindrome problem?
39   ├── YES → Hybrid approach needed
40   │   ├── Multiple patterns combined → Use multiple templates
41   │   ├── Additional constraints → Modify existing templates
42   │   └── Novel problem type → Design custom solution
43   └── NO → Re-analyze problem requirements

Template Selection Guide

When to Use Each Template:

Template 1 - Two Pointers:

  • ✅ Simple palindrome validation
  • ✅ Problems with character filtering (spaces, punctuation)
  • ✅ Linked list palindrome checks
  • ✅ When O(1) space is required
  • ❌ Need to find all palindromic substrings
  • ❌ Complex counting/construction problems

Template 2 - Center Expansion:

  • ✅ Find longest palindromic substring
  • ✅ Count palindromic substrings
  • ✅ When you need actual substring positions
  • ✅ O(1) space constraint for counting
  • ❌ Subsequence problems (non-contiguous)
  • ❌ Complex edit distance scenarios

Template 3 - Dynamic Programming:

  • ✅ Count problems with overlapping subproblems
  • ✅ Minimum operations to make palindrome
  • ✅ Multiple queries on same string
  • ✅ Complex constraint combinations
  • ❌ Simple validation (overkill)
  • ❌ When space is very limited

Template 4 - Backtracking:

  • ✅ Generate all palindrome partitions
  • ✅ Find specific partition patterns
  • ✅ Constraint satisfaction problems
  • ✅ When you need all possible solutions
  • ❌ Only counting solutions (use DP)
  • ❌ Very large input sizes (exponential time)

Template 5 - DP Subsequence:

  • ✅ Longest palindromic subsequence
  • ✅ Minimum edit operations
  • ✅ Character deletion/insertion problems
  • ✅ When order matters but contiguity doesn’t
  • ❌ Contiguous substring problems
  • ❌ Simple validation tasks

Template 6 - Number Palindrome:

  • ✅ Integer palindrome validation
  • ✅ Mathematical palindrome construction
  • ✅ Number theory related problems
  • ✅ When string conversion is inefficient
  • ❌ String-based palindrome problems
  • ❌ Complex character manipulations

Problem-Solving Strategy

Step-by-Step Approach:

  1. Identify the Core Task:

    • Validation vs. Finding vs. Counting vs. Construction
    • Single result vs. All possible results

  2. Analyze Input Constraints:

    • String, Array, Number, or Linked List?
    • Size constraints (affects algorithm choice)
    • Special characters or filtering needed?

  3. Determine Output Requirements:

    • Boolean result, count, actual palindromes, or positions?
    • Single answer or all possible answers?

  4. Choose Base Template:

    • Use decision flowchart above
    • Consider time/space complexity requirements

  5. Adapt Template for Specifics:

    • Add filtering logic if needed
    • Modify for case sensitivity requirements
    • Add memoization if overlapping subproblems exist

  6. Optimize if Necessary:

    • Space optimization (2D DP → 1D DP)
    • Early termination conditions
    • Preprocessing for multiple queries

Common Problem Patterns Recognition

Pattern Signals:

  • “Check if palindrome” → Two Pointers
  • “Longest palindromic substring” → Center Expansion
  • “Count palindromic substrings” → Center Expansion or DP
  • “All palindrome partitions” → Backtracking
  • “Minimum insertions/deletions” → DP Subsequence
  • “Palindrome number” → Number Palindrome
  • “Can be made palindrome with k changes” → DP with constraints

Summary & Quick Reference

Complexity Quick Reference

Template Time Complexity Space Complexity Best For Notes
Two Pointers O(n) O(1) Basic validation Most efficient for simple checks
Center Expansion O(n²) O(1) Substring problems Good balance of time/space
Reverse & Compare O(n) O(n) Simple problems Easy to implement
DP (2D) O(n²) O(n²) Complex counting Handles overlapping subproblems
DP Subsequence O(n²) O(n²) → O(n) Edit distance type Space optimizable
Backtracking O(n·2^n) O(n) Generate partitions Exponential time
Number Palindrome O(log n) O(1) Integer problems Avoid string conversion

Template Quick Reference

Template Pattern Key Code Snippet
Two Pointers Validation while left < right: check s[left] == s[right]
Center Expansion Substring expand_around_center(left, right)
DP Counting Complex counting dp[i][j] = (s[i] == s[j]) and dp[i+1][j-1]
Backtracking All partitions if is_palindrome(start, end): backtrack(end+1)
DP Subsequence LPS/Edit distance dp[i][j] = dp[i+1][j-1] + 2 if s[i] == s[j]
Number Math Integer palindrome reversed_half = reversed_half * 10 + x % 10

Common Patterns & Tricks

Two Pointers Variations

1# Basic two pointers
2def is_palindrome_basic(s):
3    left, right = 0, len(s) - 1
4    while left < right:
5        if s[left] != s[right]:
6            return False
7        left += 1
8        right -= 1
9    return True
10
11# With character filtering
12def is_palindrome_alphanumeric(s):
13    left, right = 0, len(s) - 1
14    while left < right:
15        while left < right and not s[left].isalnum():
16            left += 1
17        while left < right and not s[right].isalnum():
18            right -= 1
19        if s[left].lower() != s[right].lower():
20            return False
21        left += 1
22        right -= 1
23    return True
24
25# Allow one mismatch
26def is_palindrome_one_delete(s):
27    def helper(left, right, deleted):
28        while left < right:
29            if s[left] != s[right]:
30                if deleted:  # Already used one delete
31                    return False
32                # Try deleting left or right character
33                return (helper(left + 1, right, True) or 
34                       helper(left, right - 1, True))
35            left += 1
36            right -= 1
37        return True
38    return helper(0, len(s) - 1, False)

Center Expansion Patterns

1# Standard center expansion
2def expand_around_center(s, left, right):
3    while left >= 0 and right < len(s) and s[left] == s[right]:
4        left -= 1
5        right += 1
6    return right - left - 1
7
8# With counting
9def count_palindromes_at_center(s, left, right):
10    count = 0
11    while left >= 0 and right < len(s) and s[left] == s[right]:
12        count += 1
13        left -= 1
14        right += 1
15    return count
16
17# For all centers
18def find_all_palindromes(s):
19    palindromes = []
20    for i in range(len(s)):
21        # Odd length
22        left, right = i, i
23        while left >= 0 and right < len(s) and s[left] == s[right]:
24            palindromes.append(s[left:right+1])
25            left -= 1
26            right += 1
27        # Even length
28        left, right = i, i + 1
29        while left >= 0 and right < len(s) and s[left] == s[right]:
30            palindromes.append(s[left:right+1])
31            left -= 1
32            right += 1
33    return palindromes

DP Optimization Patterns

1# Space optimization: 2D DP to 1D
2def longest_palindromic_subsequence_1D(s):
3    n = len(s)
4    dp = [1] * n  # dp[j] represents length for current i to j
5    
6    for i in range(n - 2, -1, -1):
7        prev = 0  # dp[i+1][j-1]
8        for j in range(i + 1, n):
9            temp = dp[j]  # Save current dp[j] for next iteration's prev
10            if s[i] == s[j]:
11                dp[j] = prev + 2
12            else:
13                dp[j] = max(dp[j], dp[j-1])
14            prev = temp
15    
16    return dp[n-1]
17
18# Preprocessing palindrome table
19def precompute_palindromes(s):
20    n = len(s)
21    is_palin = [[False] * n for _ in range(n)]
22    
23    # Single characters
24    for i in range(n):
25        is_palin[i][i] = True
26    
27    # Two characters
28    for i in range(n - 1):
29        is_palin[i][i+1] = (s[i] == s[i+1])
30    
31    # Three+ characters
32    for length in range(3, n + 1):
33        for i in range(n - length + 1):
34            j = i + length - 1
35            is_palin[i][j] = (s[i] == s[j] and is_palin[i+1][j-1])
36    
37    return is_palin

Problem-Solving Steps

  1. Problem Analysis

    • Read problem carefully and identify key requirements
    • Determine if it’s validation, finding, counting, or construction
    • Note constraints (case sensitivity, special characters, etc.)

  2. Pattern Recognition

    • Use the pattern signals to identify the likely approach
    • Check the decision flowchart for template selection
    • Consider hybrid approaches for complex problems

  3. Template Selection

    • Choose the most appropriate template based on requirements
    • Consider time/space complexity constraints
    • Plan for edge cases and optimizations

  4. Implementation Strategy

    • Start with the basic template structure
    • Add problem-specific modifications
    • Handle edge cases (empty string, single character, etc.)

  5. Testing & Optimization

    • Test with provided examples
    • Consider edge cases: “”, “a”, “ab”, “aba”
    • Optimize space if needed (2D DP → 1D DP)

Common Mistakes & Tips

Common Mistakes:

  • Off-by-one errors in center expansion bounds checking
  • Case sensitivity not handled when problem requires it
  • Character filtering logic incorrect for alphanumeric checks
  • Space complexity not optimized when required
  • Integer overflow in number palindrome problems
  • Backtracking without proper base case or pruning
  • DP state definition incorrect for subsequence problems

Best Practices:

  • Always check bounds before accessing array elements
  • Handle empty input and single character cases separately
  • Use consistent naming for left/right or start/end pointers
  • Comment your approach clearly, especially for DP transitions
  • Consider space optimization after getting correct solution
  • Test edge cases thoroughly before submitting
  • Use helper functions to keep main logic clean and readable

Interview Tips

  1. Start with Clarification

    • Ask about case sensitivity requirements
    • Clarify if spaces/punctuation should be ignored
    • Understand the expected output format

  2. Discuss Approach

    • Explain your chosen template and reasoning
    • Mention time/space complexity upfront
    • Discuss potential optimizations

  3. Code Systematically

    • Start with basic structure, then add details
    • Handle edge cases explicitly
    • Use meaningful variable names

  4. Test Thoroughly

    • Walk through examples step by step
    • Test edge cases: “”, “a”, “ab”, “aba”, “abcba”
    • Verify your complexity analysis

  5. Common Follow-ups

    • “Can you optimize space complexity?”
    • “What if we allow k character mismatches?”
    • “How would you handle very large inputs?”
    • “Can you solve it without extra space?”

Related Topics

  • Two Pointers: General two-pointer techniques and applications
  • Dynamic Programming: Edit distance, longest common subsequence
  • String Algorithms: Pattern matching, string manipulation
  • Backtracking: Constraint satisfaction, combinatorial problems
  • Hash Tables: Character frequency counting, anagram problems
  • Sliding Window: Substring problems with constraints

LC Examples

2-1) Longest Palindromic Substring (LC 5) — Expand Around Center

For each center (char or between chars), expand outward while palindrome holds.

1// LC 5 - Longest Palindromic Substring
2// IDEA: Expand around center — O(N^2) with O(1) space
3// time = O(N^2), space = O(1)
4public String longestPalindrome(String s) {
5    int start = 0, maxLen = 1;
6    for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
7        // odd length
8        int len1 = expand(s, i, i);
9        // even length
10        int len2 = expand(s, i, i + 1);
11        int len = Math.max(len1, len2);
12        if (len > maxLen) {
13            maxLen = len;
14            start = i - (len - 1) / 2;
15        }
16    }
17    return s.substring(start, start + maxLen);
18}
19private int expand(String s, int l, int r) {
20    while (l >= 0 && r < s.length() && s.charAt(l) == s.charAt(r)) { l--; r++; }
21    return r - l - 1;
22}

2-2) Valid Palindrome (LC 125) — Two Pointers

Skip non-alphanumeric characters and compare from both ends.

1// LC 125 - Valid Palindrome
2// IDEA: Two pointers — skip non-alphanumeric, compare chars
3// time = O(N), space = O(1)
4public boolean isPalindrome(String s) {
5    int l = 0, r = s.length() - 1;
6    while (l < r) {
7        while (l < r && !Character.isLetterOrDigit(s.charAt(l))) l++;
8        while (l < r && !Character.isLetterOrDigit(s.charAt(r))) r--;
9        if (Character.toLowerCase(s.charAt(l)) != Character.toLowerCase(s.charAt(r))) return false;
10        l++; r--;
11    }
12    return true;
13}

2-3) Palindromic Substrings (LC 647) — Count All Palindromes

Expand around each center; count every valid palindrome expansion.

1// LC 647 - Palindromic Substrings
2// IDEA: Expand around center — count all palindromes
3// time = O(N^2), space = O(1)
4public int countSubstrings(String s) {
5    int count = 0;
6    for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
7        count += expand(s, i, i);     // odd length
8        count += expand(s, i, i + 1); // even length
9    }
10    return count;
11}
12private int expand(String s, int l, int r) {
13    int cnt = 0;
14    while (l >= 0 && r < s.length() && s.charAt(l) == s.charAt(r)) {
15        cnt++; l--; r++;
16    }
17    return cnt;
18}

2-4) Palindrome Number (LC 9) — Math Reversal

Reverse second half of number and compare; negative or trailing-zero numbers are not palindromes.

1// LC 9 - Palindrome Number
2// IDEA: Reverse second half; compare with first half (avoids string conversion)
3// time = O(log N), space = O(1)
4public boolean isPalindrome(int x) {
5    if (x < 0 || (x % 10 == 0 && x != 0)) return false;
6    int rev = 0;
7    while (x > rev) { rev = rev * 10 + x % 10; x /= 10; }
8    return x == rev || x == rev / 10;  // even/odd length
9}

2-5) Valid Palindrome II (LC 680) — Two Pointers with One Skip

Two pointers; when mismatch, try skipping left or right character and check rest.

1// LC 680 - Valid Palindrome II
2// IDEA: Two pointers — on mismatch, try skipping left or right character
3// time = O(N), space = O(1)
4public boolean validPalindrome(String s) {
5    int l = 0, r = s.length() - 1;
6    while (l < r) {
7        if (s.charAt(l) != s.charAt(r))
8            return isPalin(s, l+1, r) || isPalin(s, l, r-1);
9        l++; r--;
10    }
11    return true;
12}
13private boolean isPalin(String s, int l, int r) {
14    while (l < r) { if (s.charAt(l++) != s.charAt(r--)) return false; }
15    return true;
16}

2-6) Palindrome Linked List (LC 234) — Fast/Slow Pointer + Reverse

Find middle with slow/fast pointers; reverse second half; compare both halves.

1// LC 234 - Palindrome Linked List
2// IDEA: Find mid (slow/fast), reverse second half, compare with first half
3// time = O(N), space = O(1)
4public boolean isPalindrome(ListNode head) {
5    ListNode slow = head, fast = head;
6    while (fast != null && fast.next != null) { slow = slow.next; fast = fast.next.next; }
7    ListNode rev = reverse(slow);
8    ListNode p = head, q = rev;
9    while (q != null) { if (p.val != q.val) return false; p = p.next; q = q.next; }
10    return true;
11}
12private ListNode reverse(ListNode head) {
13    ListNode prev = null;
14    while (head != null) { ListNode next = head.next; head.next = prev; prev = head; head = next; }
15    return prev;
16}

2-7) Longest Palindromic Subsequence (LC 516) — Interval DP

dp[i][j] = LPS length in s[i…j]; match endpoints add 2, else take max of subproblems.

1// LC 516 - Longest Palindromic Subsequence
2// IDEA: Interval DP — dp[i][j] = LPS length of s[i..j]
3// time = O(N^2), space = O(N^2)
4public int longestPalindromeSubseq(String s) {
5    int n = s.length();
6    int[][] dp = new int[n][n];
7    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) dp[i][i] = 1;
8    for (int len = 2; len <= n; len++)
9        for (int i = 0; i <= n - len; i++) {
10            int j = i + len - 1;
11            dp[i][j] = s.charAt(i) == s.charAt(j)
12                ? dp[i+1][j-1] + 2
13                : Math.max(dp[i+1][j], dp[i][j-1]);
14        }
15    return dp[0][n-1];
16}

2-8) Minimum Insertion Steps to Make a String Palindrome (LC 1312) — Interval DP

Minimum insertions = N − LPS length; equivalent to inserting to make palindrome.

1// LC 1312 - Minimum Insertion Steps to Make String Palindrome
2// IDEA: minInsertions = s.length() - LPS(s)
3// time = O(N^2), space = O(N^2)
4public int minInsertions(String s) {
5    int n = s.length();
6    int[][] dp = new int[n][n];
7    for (int len = 2; len <= n; len++)
8        for (int i = 0; i <= n - len; i++) {
9            int j = i + len - 1;
10            dp[i][j] = s.charAt(i) == s.charAt(j)
11                ? dp[i+1][j-1]
12                : Math.min(dp[i+1][j], dp[i][j-1]) + 1;
13        }
14    return dp[0][n-1];
15}

2-9) Palindrome Partitioning (LC 131) — Backtracking

DFS with backtracking; at each index try all palindromic prefixes as next partition.

1// LC 131 - Palindrome Partitioning
2// IDEA: Backtracking — at each position try all palindromic prefixes; recurse on suffix
3// time = O(N * 2^N), space = O(N)
4public List<List<String>> partition(String s) {
5    List<List<String>> res = new ArrayList<>();
6    backtrack(s, 0, new ArrayList<>(), res);
7    return res;
8}
9private void backtrack(String s, int start, List<String> path, List<List<String>> res) {
10    if (start == s.length()) { res.add(new ArrayList<>(path)); return; }
11    for (int end = start + 1; end <= s.length(); end++) {
12        String sub = s.substring(start, end);
13        if (isPalin(sub)) {
14            path.add(sub);
15            backtrack(s, end, path, res);
16            path.remove(path.size() - 1);
17        }
18    }
19}
20private boolean isPalin(String s) {
21    int l = 0, r = s.length() - 1;
22    while (l < r) if (s.charAt(l++) != s.charAt(r--)) return false;
23    return true;
24}

2-10) Palindrome Partitioning II (LC 132) — DP

dp[i] = minimum cuts for s[0…i]; precompute palindrome table or expand-around-center.

1// LC 132 - Palindrome Partitioning II
2// IDEA: DP — dp[i] = min cuts for s[0..i]; precompute isPalin table
3// time = O(N^2), space = O(N^2)
4public int minCut(String s) {
5    int n = s.length();
6    boolean[][] isPalin = new boolean[n][n];
7    for (int i = n-1; i >= 0; i--)
8        for (int j = i; j < n; j++)
9            isPalin[i][j] = s.charAt(i) == s.charAt(j) && (j - i < 2 || isPalin[i+1][j-1]);
10    int[] dp = new int[n];
11    Arrays.fill(dp, Integer.MAX_VALUE);
12    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
13        if (isPalin[0][i]) { dp[i] = 0; continue; }
14        for (int j = 1; j <= i; j++)
15            if (isPalin[j][i]) dp[i] = Math.min(dp[i], dp[j-1] + 1);
16    }
17    return dp[n-1];
18}

2-11) Find Longest Awesome Substring (LC 1542) — Bitmask + Prefix XOR

Awesome = at most one character with odd frequency; track prefix XOR states.

1// LC 1542 - Find Longest Awesome Substring
2// IDEA: Prefix XOR bitmask; awesome substring iff XOR has at most 1 set bit
3// time = O(10 * N), space = O(1024)
4public int longestAwesome(String s) {
5    int[] seen = new int[1024];
6    Arrays.fill(seen, s.length());
7    seen[0] = -1;
8    int prefix = 0, ans = 0;
9    for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
10        prefix ^= 1 << (s.charAt(i) - '0');
11        if (seen[prefix] <= s.length()) ans = Math.max(ans, i - seen[prefix]);
12        else seen[prefix] = i;
13        for (int d = 0; d <= 9; d++) {  // try one odd-count digit
14            int mask = prefix ^ (1 << d);
15            if (seen[mask] <= s.length()) ans = Math.max(ans, i - seen[mask]);
16        }
17    }
18    return ans;
19}
  • Tree Algorithms: Path problems, symmetric tree validation
← Back to Cheat Sheets Edit on GitHub →
← N SumPrefix Sum →

CS_basics - Computer Science Fundamentals & Interview Preparation

GitHub • Documentation • Report Issues
